questions.questionText: The most effective reversible long-acting contraceptive method is:
questions.optionB: LARC — Intrauterine devices (copper IUD/LNG-IUS) and subdermal implants (failure rate <1%)
questions.explanation: LARC (Long-Acting Reversible Contraception): most effective reversible methods (failure rate <1%). Copper IUD (Cu-T 380A): non-hormonal, effective 10 years, prevents fertilization (spermicidal copper ions) and implantation. LNG-IUS (Mirena): releases levonorgestrel, 5 years, thins endometrium, thickens cervical mucus. Subdermal implant (Implanon/Nexplanon): etonogestrel, 3 years, most effective reversible contraceptive (failure rate 0.05%). Emergency contraception: levonorgestrel 1.5mg within 72h, or Cu-IUD within 5 days. Reference: DC Dutta — Contraception.

questions.questionText: The mechanism of action of combined oral contraceptive pills (COC) is primarily:
questions.optionB: Suppression of ovulation by inhibiting GnRH/FSH/LH surge + thickening cervical mucus + thinning endometrium
questions.explanation: COC (estrogen + progestin): primary mechanism — suppression of ovulation (inhibits GnRH pulsatility → suppresses FSH/LH surge → no dominant follicle/ovulation). Secondary: thickens cervical mucus (sperm barrier), thins endometrium (impairs implantation), alters tubal motility. Non-contraceptive benefits: menstrual regulation, reduced dysmenorrhea, protection against ovarian/endometrial cancer, treatment of acne/hirsutism. Absolute contraindications: >35 years + smoker, history of DVT/PE, migraine with aura, breast cancer, liver disease. Reference: DC Dutta — OCP.
